name: Sourdough Sams
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the dry ingredients: active sourdough starter, nutmeg, sugar, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the buttermilk (or sour milk), egg, and baking powder with the shortening until smooth.
3. Gradually add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring until just combined.
4. Shape the dough into balls and chill for 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
5. Preheat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 390°F (200°C).
6. Roll out chilled dough between two sheets of flour on a lightly floured surface until about 1/2 inch thick.
7. Cut with a donut cutter and place into the hot oil.
8. Fry until golden brown, turning once halfway through cooking, for about 2 minutes per side.
9. Remove doughnuts with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
10. Dust with powdered sugar or cinnamon sugar before serving.
11. Store leftover doughnuts in an airtight container at room temperature; they keep well for up to three days.
